title: Garlicky Shattering Algerian Salt Cod Dolsot
description: A hearty, flavorful Algerian-inspired dish featuring shattering salt cod in a spicy garlic broth, served in a stone bowl. Flexitarian-friendly and perfect for a weekend brunch.
instructions: Season the salt cod fillets with salt and black pepper.
Drizzle the olive oil in the bottom of a 3-quart Dutch oven or heavy saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the cumin, coriander, and crushed red pepper and stir for 1 minute.
Add the salt cod fillets to the pot and pour in enough water to cover them.
Bring the water to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the fish flakes easily with a fork.
While the fish is cooking, heat the lemon juice in a small saucepan over low heat.
Remove the fish from the oven and carefully place it on top of the cooked onion mixture.
Strain the cooking liquid and discard the solids.
Pour the cooking liquid over the fish and serve hot, garnished with chopped parsley and cilantro.
To serve, place a portion of the fish and onion mixture in the center of a hot stone bowl and pour the remaining cooking liquid around it.
Serve immediately, with crusty bread or over rice if desired.
